title: feat(typescript): allow generics for the Hit type in all hit-displaying connectors and widgets
body:
By leveraging `satisfies` instead of a type annotation, we can use the original typing of the widget and connector, and thus add additional generics that aren't already filled in before export.

Relevant widgets and connectors:
- hits
- infiniteHits
- geoSearch
- frequentlyBoughtTogether
- trendingItems
- lookingSimilar
- relatedProducts

Through this PR some other smaller things had to be changed around typing due to slight upgrade of api-extractor (that in the end didn't seem needed anymore). These seem like improvements to me.

There's a small downside that due to using `satisfies`, the type in the declaration is quite large, every single key of the function and widget object in the end get redeclared rather than using the XXXConnector type, as the XXXConnector can of course not have a non-specified generic (that's the whole problem this PR tries to solve).

I didn't touch connectAutocomplete (although it should work in theory too) as you could have one generic per index in a federated search context, and introducing a single generic now may make it harder later to support multiple generics.


It's not possible to provide a generic to the whole InstantSearch module (although I wish we could), but maybe in the future this problem could be solved with interface merging as well (users declare a Hit type, which merges with a Hit type from InstantSearch), but that seems like a bigger task than supporting generics properly here.

Review tips:
- use "hide whitespace" setting on GitHub to see much less diffs that are mainly caused by different indentation
- there's a lot of repetition, I recommend you ensure it's right in one of the widgets/connectors, then compare if it's the same for all the other widgets/connectors (there's only very little difference)

FX-2908
